title: Safety and Efficacy of Angiotensin (1-7) in Persons With Moderate to Severe Traumatic Brain Injury

skipped_criteria: Traumatically induced head injury from external force: partially covered by presumed_tbi but mechanism specificity (external force to head) cannot be fully expressed with available fields.
Clinical diagnosis of acute intracranial lesion based on neuroradiologist CT report: requires imaging/radiology report data not available in schema.
Moderate TBI defined as loss of consciousness 30 min–24 hours with GCS 9–12; Severe TBI defined as LOC >24 hours with GCS ≤9: loss-of-consciousness duration is not a schema field.
Enrollment within 48 hours of TBI: temporal criterion (time since injury) cannot be expressed with available schema fields.
Time of injury cannot be determined (exclusion): temporal/administrative criterion not representable in schema.
Neurosurgery within the last 30 days (exclusion): prior surgical history is not a schema field.
History of neurodegenerative disease or disorder including dementia, Parkinson's, MS, seizure disorder, or brain tumors (exclusion): prior medical history not available in schema.
Contraindication to MRI (exclusion): imaging contraindication is not a schema field.
Lactating female (exclusion): lactation status is not a schema field.
Female of childbearing potential or sexually active male unwilling to use birth control (exclusion): contraceptive compliance is not a schema field.
Participation in another clinical study involving investigational product within 30 days (exclusion): prior trial participation is not a schema field.
Investigator opinion of unsuitability (exclusion): subjective investigator judgment cannot be encoded in schema.
